MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Lysle Kapp, Esq., of BECHERER KANNETT & SCHWEITZER (510-658-3600), appearing for defendant / moving party Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Kim Morgan

RULING:
DEFENDANT DILLINGHAM CONSTRUCTION N.A., INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. After conceding that plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue regarding plaintiff's exposure to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant, defendant's reply memorandum states that the 'sole issue ' is whether Dillingham owed a duty to warn' plaintiff about the hazards of asbestos. Defendant's argument that it lacks such a duty because plaintiff was aware of the hazards of asbestos-containing insulation and was able to recognize such insulation prior to the first time he was in the vicinity of defendant's employees disturbing asbestos-containing insulation is based on evidence submitted with defendant's reply papers. The court declines to consider such evidence because plaintiff did not have the opportunity to respond to that evidence in writing and such evidence was available to defendant at the time it filed its moving papers. Parties are to meet and confer re: plaintiff's proposed order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patrick M. Malone, Esq., and Andrea J. Casalett, Esq., Esq., of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4000), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Kim Morgan

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off the record defendant's counsel informed the Court of the submission to the tentative ruling except ruling as to summary adjudication #5. Matter argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 4 and 5 (negligence, premises owner/contractor liability and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issues 2 and 3 (strict liability and false representation). As to negligence and premises owner/contractor liability, plaintiff's deposition and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing pipe insulation attributable to defendant. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression warranting the imposition of punitive damages. Defendant's purported undisputed facts no. 44-47 fail to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to defendant's discovery seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting plaintiff's punitive damages claim, precluding defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for strict liability and false representation. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Luiza Manuelian, Esq. of POOLE & SHAFFERY, LLP (213-439-5390), appearing for defendant / moving party H&C Investment Associates, Inc.
Oren Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Kim Morgan, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT H&C INVESTMENT ASSOCIATES, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant H&C Investment Associates, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. The motion for summary judgment is denied because defendant failed to sustain its burden of production under either of the two prongs enunciated in Aguilar. By not presenting any substantive responses to contention discovery, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant and that any such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff's asbestos-related illness.. The declarations of Dr. Rabnovitz and Dr. Sandler do not negate an element of plaintiff's claims because those declarations are largely inadmissible, assume that the only percipient witness that plaintiff has to prove his claims is plaintiff himself (as to which there is no evidence), further assumes (contrary to plaintiff's deposition) that a Childers product never got on the hands of plaintiff, and still further assumes that such product was never in a dry state on plaintiff's hands (as to which there is no evidence) . Plaintiff's objections to the declarations of Dr. Rabinovitz and Dr. Sandler are sustained. The motion for summary adjudication of punitive damages is denied because defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Plaintiff's responses to contention discovery regarding punitive damages that only contain objections do not provide a basis to draw the necessary inference for the successful invocation of the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. (Gaggero v. Yura (2003) 108 Cal. App 4th 884, 892). Order signed in open court. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7707. =(220/HEK)
